title: Regime-specialist ensemble with AUC-safe monotone blending
summary: Dry mandatory-stop races, wet/intermediate races, and pre-season testing follow different strategic rules, so a single global classifier is likely averaging incompatible mechanisms.
rationale: Dry mandatory-stop races, wet/intermediate races, and pre-season testing follow different strategic rules, so a single global classifier is likely averaging incompatible mechanisms. Specialist models are a common way to exploit such heterogeneity, and monotone calibration preserves rank-sensitive metrics better than isotonic when AUC is the target.
implementation_hint: Train separate experts for at least slick-dry, wet/intermediate, and pre-season-testing regimes; optionally split dry races again by 'mandatory stop still owed' versus 'free to finish'. Blend only with blocked OOF logits using a simple logistic meta-model or rank averaging. If post-hoc calibration is used, prefer sigmoid or temperature scaling over isotonic on the final blend.
expected_effect: Should improve tail behavior in rare regimes and give a safer ensemble path than adding many more hand-built global features.
risk: Rare-regime experts can overfit, and a bad gating rule can erase gains; this works best with strict OOF discipline.
